badges.title: Badges
badges.headings: Badges What you can do Who this applies to Getting started Related sections
badges.body:
Badges
The Badges section shows all the LFX credentialing badges you have earned through your participation in Linux Foundation projects and programs. Badges are digital credentials that recognize your contributions, certifications, and involvement across the LFX platform.

What you can do

View all badges earned across your projects

See which project or program issued each badge

Access sharing and export options from the badge detail view

Who this applies to
All authenticated users can view their own badges. The badges displayed reflect activity across all projects you participate in.

Getting started
Go to app.lfx.dev and select Badges from the left navigation. Your earned badges appear in the main content area. Filter by category using the tabs: All, Certifications, Learning, Memberships, Speaking, Event Participation, Contributors, Program Committee.

Related sections

Profile — your profile overview and account details

Trainings — training enrollments that may lead to badge awards

badges/faq.title: Badges FAQ
badges/faq.headings: Badges FAQ How do I earn a badge? Where can I see all my badges? Why don't I see a badge I expected to receive? Are my badges visible to others? Can I share my badges externally? Do badges expire? Who do I contact if a badge is incorrect?
badges/faq.body:
Badges FAQ
How do I earn a badge?
Badges are awarded automatically when you meet the qualifying criteria for a Linux Foundation program, certification, or contribution milestone. You do not need to apply manually — the system grants badges based on your recorded activity and certifications.

Where can I see all my badges?
Go to Badges in the left navigation of app.lfx.dev. All badges earned across your projects appear in one list.

Why don't I see a badge I expected to receive?
There may be a delay between completing a qualifying activity and the badge appearing in your account. If a badge is missing after 24 hours, contact LFX support.

Are my badges visible to others?
Badges are associated with your LFX profile. Contact LFX support for questions about badge visibility.

Can I share my badges externally?
Badge sharing and export options vary by badge type. Select a badge to see the options available.

Do badges expire?
Some badges may have expiry dates. Check the badge detail view for expiry information.

Who do I contact if a badge is incorrect?
Contact LFX support through the Help Center. Include the badge name and the project it relates to so the support team can investigate.

badges/view-your-badges.title: View Your Badges
badges/view-your-badges.headings: View Your Badges Steps What the badges page shows Empty state Related
badges/view-your-badges.body:
View Your Badges
Your LFX badges are digital credentials that recognize your contributions and achievements across Linux Foundation projects and programs. You can view all your earned badges in one place.

Steps

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Select Badges from the left navigation sidebar.

Your earned badges appear in the main content area. Use the tabs to filter by category: All, Certifications, Learning, Memberships, Speaking, Event Participation, Contributors, Program Committee.

Select a badge to see its details, including the issuing project and the criteria met.

What the badges page shows

All badges earned across your LFX activity

The project or program that issued each badge

Select a badge to view its details, including issue information and any sharing or export options available for that badge type.

Empty state
If you have not earned any badges yet, the page displays a message indicating no badges are available. Badges are awarded automatically when you meet the qualifying criteria for a program or certification.

Related

Trainings — complete training programs that may qualify you for badges

Profile — view your overall contributor profile

committees.title: My Groups
committees.headings: My Groups What you can do Navigation Key concepts Related sections
committees.body:
My Groups
This page applies to users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas. Contributors can view committee information but cannot create or manage committees.

In LFX Self Serve, committees are displayed as Groups. Navigate to My Groups at /groups to see your committee memberships. Committees are formal groups that govern project decisions, including Technical Steering Committees (TSCs), boards, and other working groups.

What you can do

View all committees for your project or foundation

Create new committees

Edit committee details and membership

View committee members and their roles

Manage committee settings

Navigation
Go to app.lfx.dev and select Groups (route: /groups) from the left navigation. The committees dashboard lists all committees you have access to.

Key concepts

Committee: A formal group with defined membership and a governance role within a project

Committee view: The detail page for a single committee, showing its members and settings

Committee management: The create and edit workflow for committee configuration

Related sections

Votes — create and manage polls for committee decisions

Meetings — schedule and manage committee meetings

Documents — manage documents associated with committees

committees/create-committee.title: Create a Committee
committees/create-committee.headings: Create a Committee Before you begin Steps After creation Permissions Related
committees/create-committee.body:
Create a Committee
This article applies to users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director roles. You can create new committees for your project directly from the LFX Self Serve portal.

Before you begin
Make sure you have the correct project or foundation selected in the lens switcher. The new committee will be associated with the currently active project context.

Steps

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Select Groups from the left navigation sidebar.

Select the option to create a new committee (route: /groups/manage).

Fill in the required fields:
Committee name — the display name for your committee

Description — a brief explanation of the committee's purpose and scope

Add initial members if needed.

Save the committee.

After creation
The new committee appears in the committees dashboard. You can edit its details or add members at any time from the committee detail page.

Permissions
Only users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas can create committees. If you do not see the create option, your current role does not have the required access for this project context.

Related

Manage Committees — edit and update existing committees

Votes — set up votes for the new committee

committees/faq.title: Committees FAQ
committees/faq.headings: Committees FAQ Who can create a committee? Why can't I see any committees? How do I add members to a committee? Can I have the same person in multiple committees? How do I change the committee name or description? What happens to a committee's meetings and votes when the committee is deleted? Who do I contact if I need help with committees?
committees/faq.body:
Committees FAQ
Who can create a committee?
Users with a maintainer, board-member, or executive-director persona can create committees. Contributors can view committee information but cannot create or manage committees.

Why can't I see any committees?
Make sure you have the correct project or foundation selected in the lens switcher. Committees are scoped to a specific project context. If you switch to the Foundation lens, you see foundation-level committees; the Project lens shows project-level committees.

How do I add members to a committee?
Open the group from the My Groups page, navigate to the members section, and use the add member option to search and add by name or email.

Can I have the same person in multiple committees?
Yes, a single user can be a member of multiple committees within the same project or across different projects.

How do I change the committee name or description?
Open the committee detail page and select the edit option. Update the fields and save your changes.

What happens to a committee's meetings and votes when the committee is deleted?
Contact your project administrator before deleting a committee, as this may affect associated meetings, votes, and documents.

Who do I contact if I need help with committees?
Contact LFX support through the Help Center. Include your project name and the committee name in your request.

committees/manage-committees.title: Manage Committees
committees/manage-committees.headings: Manage Committees View all committees View committee details Edit a committee Add or remove members Switch project context Related
committees/manage-committees.body:
Manage Committees
This article applies to users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director roles. Use the Committees section to view committee membership, update details, and manage your project's governance groups.

View all committees

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Select Groups from the left navigation sidebar.

The committees dashboard lists all committees for your active project or foundation context.

Select a committee name to open its detail view.

View committee details
The committee detail view (route: /groups/:id) shows:

Committee name and description

Current members and their roles

Edit a committee

Open a committee from the committees dashboard.

Select the edit option from the committee detail page.

Update the committee name, description, or other fields.

Save your changes.

Add or remove members

Open the committee you want to update.

Navigate to the members section of the committee detail view.

Select the add member option, search for the person by name or email, and confirm.

Save your changes.

Switch project context
Committees are scoped to a specific project or foundation. Use the lens switcher in the top navigation to change between your Foundation, Project, or Me views. The committees list updates automatically to reflect the selected context.

Related

Create a Committee — steps to create a new committee

Votes — run polls for committee decisions

dashboards.title: Dashboard
dashboards.headings: Dashboard Lens system Persona system What you see in each lens Related sections
dashboards.body:
Dashboard
The Dashboard is the central starting point of LFX Self Serve. It adapts to show you relevant information based on who you are (your persona) and what you are viewing (your lens).

Lens system
The LFX dashboard uses a lens concept to let you switch between four different viewing perspectives:

Lens
Description

Me
Your personal activity, contributions, and involvement

Foundation
Foundation-level governance, projects, and analytics

Project
A specific project's metrics, committees, and activity

Org
Your organization's membership and participation

The lenses available to you depend on your persona. Not all users see all four lenses.

Persona system
Your persona is determined by your committee memberships and roles across Linux Foundation projects. The four personas are:

Persona
Access

Contributor
Me and Project lenses

Maintainer
Me and Project lenses with write access

Board Member
Me, Foundation, and Project lenses

Executive Director
All four lenses

What you see in each lens

Me lens (route: /) — your personal dashboard with contributions, badges, and upcoming events

Foundation lens (route: /foundation/overview) — foundation-level overview with health metrics and project summaries

Project lens (route: /project/overview) — a specific project's activity, committees, and contributor metrics

Org lens (route: /org) — your organization's membership and participation data

Related sections

Committees — manage your project governance groups

Meetings — view upcoming and past meetings

Votes — participate in and manage polls
dashboards.tags: dashboard overview lens persona me foundation project org

dashboards/faq.title: Dashboard FAQ
dashboards/faq.headings: Dashboard FAQ Why don't I see all four lenses? Why does my dashboard show different content from a colleague's? How do I change the project I'm viewing? My lens preference isn't being remembered. What should I do? What are the drawers I see when I click on dashboard cards? How do I get access to the Foundation lens? Why is the Health Metrics page not accessible to me?
dashboards/faq.body:
Dashboard FAQ
Why don't I see all four lenses?
The lenses available to you depend on your persona. Contributors and maintainers see the Me and Project lenses. Board members and executive directors additionally see the Foundation lens. Executive directors also have access to the Org lens. If you believe you should have access to a lens that is not visible, contact your foundation or project administrator.

Why does my dashboard show different content from a colleague's?
The dashboard adapts to your persona and the active project context. If you and your colleague have different personas or have selected different projects, your dashboards will look different.

How do I change the project I'm viewing?
Use the project context picker in the top navigation when you are in the Project or Foundation lens. Select a different project or foundation to update the entire page.

My lens preference isn't being remembered. What should I do?
LFX Self Serve saves your lens selection in a browser cookie that lasts 30 days. If you are using private/incognito mode or your browser blocks cookies, the preference will not persist. Try using a standard browser session.

What are the drawers I see when I click on dashboard cards?
Drawers are sliding side panels that show additional detail — such as metrics charts or related items — without navigating away from the dashboard. Close a drawer by selecting the close icon or pressing Escape.

How do I get access to the Foundation lens?
The Foundation lens is available to users with a board-member or executive-director persona. Personas are determined by your committee memberships. Contact your project administrator if you need elevated access.

Why is the Health Metrics page not accessible to me?
The /foundation/health-metrics page requires an executive-director persona. If you see an access denied message, your current persona does not include this role.

dashboards/navigate-dashboards.title: Navigate Your Dashboard
dashboards/navigate-dashboards.headings: Navigate Your Dashboard Switch lenses Me lens Foundation lens Project lens Org lens Switch project context Drawers and detail panels Related
dashboards/navigate-dashboards.body:
Navigate Your Dashboard
The LFX Self Serve dashboard adapts based on your persona and the lens you have selected. This article explains how to switch lenses, what each view contains, and how to use the project context switcher.

Switch lenses

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Locate the lens switcher in the top navigation or sidebar.

Select one of the available lenses: Me, Foundation, Project, or Org.

The dashboard and left navigation update automatically to reflect the selected lens.

Your lens selection is saved for 30 days. The next time you sign in, the app restores your last-used lens.

Me lens
The Me lens (the default view at /) shows your personal activity across all your projects. You see your recent contributions, upcoming meetings, earned badges, and training enrollments.

Foundation lens
The Foundation lens (/foundation/overview) is available to board members and executive directors. It shows foundation-level health metrics, project summaries, and governance activity. Executive directors also see additional health metrics at /foundation/health-metrics.

Project lens
The Project lens (/project/overview) focuses on a single project. Select the project you want to view from the project context picker. The view updates to show that project's committees, meetings, votes, and contributor metrics.

Org lens
The Org lens shows your organization's membership status and participation across Linux Foundation projects.

Switch project context
When you are in the Foundation or Project lens, you can switch between different projects or foundations using the context picker in the top navigation. Select a different foundation or project to update the entire page to that scope.

Drawers and detail panels
Many dashboard cards open a drawer (a side panel) when you select them. Drawers show detailed metrics, charts, or related items without leaving the current page. Close a drawer by selecting the close icon or pressing Escape.

Related

Dashboard overview — lens and persona concepts

Committees — manage your committees from the dashboard

documents.title: Documents
documents.headings: Documents What you can do Navigation Project context Related sections
documents.body:
Documents
This page applies to users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas.

The Documents section lets you browse and manage documents associated with your Linux Foundation projects. Documents typically include governance materials, meeting notes, policy files, and other project-related content.

What you can do

Browse documents for your project or foundation

View document details

Navigation
Go to app.lfx.dev and select Documents from the left navigation sidebar. The documents dashboard lists all documents available for your current project or foundation context. Use the tabs to filter: All Sources, Links, Meetings, Mailing Lists.

Project context
Documents are scoped to a project or foundation. Use the lens switcher to change your active context and see documents for a different project or foundation.

Related sections

Committees — committees often have associated documents

Meetings — meeting agendas and notes may be stored as documents

documents/faq.title: Documents FAQ
documents/faq.headings: Documents FAQ Who can see project documents? Why don't I see any documents? Can I download documents? Can I upload documents? Who do I contact if a document is missing or incorrect? Are documents shared across projects?
documents/faq.body:
Documents FAQ
Who can see project documents?
Document visibility depends on your persona. If you cannot see expected documents, contact your project administrator.

Why don't I see any documents?
Make sure you have the correct project or foundation selected in the lens switcher. Documents are scoped to a specific context. If the correct context is selected and you still see no documents, your project may not have any documents uploaded yet.

Can I download documents?
Select a document to open its detail view and access available download or view options.

Can I upload documents?
Document upload availability depends on your project role. Contact your project administrator if you need upload access.

Who do I contact if a document is missing or incorrect?
Contact your project administrator or LFX support through the Help Center. Include the document name and the project it belongs to.

Are documents shared across projects?
No, documents are scoped to a specific project or foundation. A document uploaded for one project is not visible to other projects unless explicitly shared.

documents/manage-documents.title: Manage Documents
documents/manage-documents.headings: Manage Documents Browse documents Filter and search documents View document details Upload a document Switch project context Related
documents/manage-documents.body:
Manage Documents
This article applies to users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas. Use the Documents section to find and work with files associated with your project.

Browse documents

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Select Documents from the left navigation sidebar.

The documents dashboard shows all documents available for your current project context.

Select a document to view its details.

Filter and search documents
Use the All Sources, Links, Meetings, and Mailing Lists tabs to filter documents by source.

View document details
Select a document from the list to open its detail view. The detail view shows:

Document name and description

Upload a document
Document upload availability depends on your project role. If upload is available, use the upload option on the My Documents page.

Switch project context
Documents are scoped to a specific project or foundation. Use the lens switcher in the top navigation to change your active project or foundation. The documents list updates automatically.

Related

Documents overview — an overview of the Documents section

Meetings — meeting agendas are often stored as documents

events.title: Events
events.headings: Events What you can do Who this applies to Navigation Lens context Related sections
events.body:
Events
The Events section shows Linux Foundation events relevant to you and your projects. You can browse upcoming events, view event details, and manage your attendance.

What you can do

Browse upcoming and past LFX events

View event details including dates, location, and description

The Events page shows events you are already registered for. Registration is typically handled on the event's external registration page.

Filter using the All Foundations, All Roles, and All Statuses dropdowns. Switch between Upcoming, Past, Visa Letters, and Travel Funding tabs.

Who this applies to
All authenticated users can browse events. Event visibility may vary based on your project affiliations and the active lens.

Navigation
Go to app.lfx.dev and select Events from the left navigation sidebar. The events page lists upcoming events relevant to your projects and profile.

Lens context
The events shown may be filtered by your active lens and project context. Switch between the Me, Foundation, Project, and Org lenses to see events relevant to each scope.

Related sections

Meetings — internal project meetings separate from public LFX events

Dashboard — upcoming events may appear on your personal dashboard

events/browse-events.title: Browse Events
events/browse-events.headings: Browse Events Steps What an event listing shows Filter events Register for an event Empty state Related
events/browse-events.body:
Browse Events
Use the Events section to find Linux Foundation events relevant to your projects and interests.

Steps

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Select Events from the left navigation sidebar.

Browse the list of upcoming and past events.

Select an event to view its full details.

What an event listing shows
The events table shows the following columns:

Event Name

Foundation — the foundation associated with the event

Date

Location

Role

Status

Registration is handled externally. The Events page displays events you are already registered for.

Filter events
Filter by All Foundations, All Roles, All Statuses using the dropdown menus. Use the tabs to switch between Upcoming, Past, Visa Letters, and Travel Funding.

Register for an event
LFX Self Serve shows your registered events. To register for new events, visit the event's external registration page.

Empty state
If no events appear, your current project context may not have any scheduled events. Try switching your lens to see events for a different scope, or check back later.

Related

Events overview — an overview of the Events section

Meetings — for internal project meetings, go to the Meetings section

events/faq.title: Events FAQ
events/faq.headings: Events FAQ What is the difference between Events and Meetings? Why don't I see any events? Can I register for events from LFX Self Serve? Can I filter events by project or date? Can I see events I attended in the past? How do I get notified about new events? Who do I contact about a specific event?
events/faq.body:
Events FAQ
What is the difference between Events and Meetings?
Events are Linux Foundation public conferences, summits, and community gatherings. Meetings are internal project meetings such as Technical Steering Committee (TSC) calls and working group sessions. Use the Events section for public LFX events and the Meetings section for project-scoped calls.

Why don't I see any events?
Your current project context or lens may not have any scheduled events. Try switching to the Me lens to see events across all your affiliations. Events may also be added on a rolling basis as they are scheduled.

Can I register for events from LFX Self Serve?
Event registration is handled externally (not within LFX Self Serve). My Events shows events you are already registered for across all foundations. To register for a new event, visit the event's external registration page.

Can I filter events by project or date?
Filter using the All Foundations, All Roles, and All Statuses dropdowns. Tabs include Upcoming, Past, Visa Letters, and Travel Funding.

Can I see events I attended in the past?
Yes. Select the Past tab on the My Events page to view events you have previously attended.

How do I get notified about new events?
Check the My Events page regularly for new events. Contact your project administrator for information about event announcements in your project.

Who do I contact about a specific event?
Contact the event organizer listed on the event detail page, or reach out to LFX support through the Help Center.

mailing-lists.title: Mailing Lists
mailing-lists.headings: Mailing Lists What you can do Who this applies to Navigation Key concepts Related sections
mailing-lists.body:
Mailing Lists
The Mailing Lists section lets you view and manage email mailing lists associated with your Linux Foundation projects. Mailing lists are the primary communication channel for many open source project communities.

What you can do

View mailing lists for your project or foundation

Subscribe to or unsubscribe from mailing lists

Create new mailing lists (maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas)

View mailing list details and subscriber counts

Manage mailing list settings

Who this applies to
All authenticated users can browse and subscribe to mailing lists. Creating and managing mailing lists requires a maintainer, board-member, or executive-director persona.

Navigation
Go to app.lfx.dev and select Mailing Lists from the left navigation sidebar. The mailing lists dashboard (route: /mailing-lists) shows all lists available for your current project context.

Key concepts

Mailing list: An email distribution group for project communications

Subscriber: A member who receives messages sent to the list

List settings: Configuration options such as moderation and archiving

Related sections

Committees — committees often have associated mailing lists

Meetings — meeting invitations are often sent through mailing lists

mailing-lists/create-mailing-list.title: Create a Mailing List
mailing-lists/create-mailing-list.headings: Create a Mailing List Before you begin Steps After creation Permissions Related
mailing-lists/create-mailing-list.body:
Create a Mailing List
This article applies to users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas. You can create new mailing lists for your project to support community communications.

Before you begin
Confirm you have the correct project selected in the lens switcher. The new mailing list will be associated with the currently active project context.

Steps

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Select Mailing Lists from the left navigation sidebar.

Select the option to create a new mailing list (route: /mailing-lists/manage).

Fill in the required fields:
List name — the display name and identifier for the mailing list

Description — a brief explanation of the list's purpose

Save the mailing list.

After creation
The new mailing list appears in the mailing lists dashboard. Community members can subscribe to it from the list detail page. You can edit the list settings at any time.

Permissions
Only users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas can create mailing lists. If you do not see the create option, contact your project administrator.

Related

Manage Mailing Lists — edit and update existing lists

Committees — link committees with mailing lists for community communication

mailing-lists/faq.title: Mailing Lists FAQ
mailing-lists/faq.headings: Mailing Lists FAQ Who can subscribe to a mailing list? Who can create a mailing list? Why don't I see any mailing lists? How do I unsubscribe from a mailing list? What happens to a mailing list when the project is archived? How do I change the mailing list name or description? Who do I contact if a mailing list is set up incorrectly?
mailing-lists/faq.body:
Mailing Lists FAQ
Who can subscribe to a mailing list?
All authenticated LFX users can subscribe to project mailing lists. Some lists may be restricted to project members or have moderation enabled.

Who can create a mailing list?
Users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas can create mailing lists. Contributors can subscribe to existing lists but cannot create new ones.

Why don't I see any mailing lists?
Confirm your active project context in the lens switcher. Mailing lists are scoped to a specific project or foundation. If the correct project is selected and you still see no lists, the project may not have created any mailing lists yet.

How do I unsubscribe from a mailing list?
Open the mailing list from the Mailing Lists section and select the unsubscribe option on the list detail page.

What happens to a mailing list when the project is archived?
If a project is archived, contact LFX support for information about accessing its mailing list history.

How do I change the mailing list name or description?
Open the mailing list detail page and navigate to the manage or settings view. Update the fields and save your changes. This requires a maintainer or higher persona.

Who do I contact if a mailing list is set up incorrectly?
Contact LFX support through the Help Center. Include the list name and the project it belongs to.

mailing-lists/manage-mailing-lists.title: Manage Mailing Lists
mailing-lists/manage-mailing-lists.headings: Manage Mailing Lists View mailing lists Subscribe to a mailing list Unsubscribe from a mailing list Manage list settings (maintainers and administrators) Switch project context Related
mailing-lists/manage-mailing-lists.body:
Manage Mailing Lists
Use this page to subscribe to project mailing lists, manage your subscriptions, and (if you have the required role) update list settings.

View mailing lists

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Select Mailing Lists from the left navigation sidebar.

The mailing lists dashboard shows all lists for your active project context.

Select a mailing list to open its detail view (route: /mailing-lists/:id).

Subscribe to a mailing list

Open the mailing list you want to join.

Select the subscribe option on the list detail page.

After subscribing, check your email for a confirmation message from the mailing list.

Unsubscribe from a mailing list

Open the mailing list you want to leave.

Select the unsubscribe option.

Confirm your choice when prompted.

Manage list settings (maintainers and administrators)

Open the mailing list you want to manage.

Navigate to the manage or settings view (route: /mailing-lists/:id/manage).

Update the list name, description, or moderation settings as needed.

Save your changes.

Switch project context
Mailing lists are scoped to a project or foundation. Use the lens switcher to change your active context and see lists for a different project.

Related

Create a Mailing List — steps to create a new list

Mailing Lists overview — overview of the Mailing Lists section

meetings.title: Meetings
meetings.headings: Meetings What you can do Who this applies to Navigation Key concepts Public meeting access Related sections
meetings.body:
Meetings
The Meetings section lets you create, manage, and join meetings for your Linux Foundation project groups. Meetings include Technical Steering Committee (TSC) calls, working group sessions, and other recurring or one-time project gatherings.

What you can do

View upcoming and past project meetings

Schedule new meetings with calendar integration

Edit existing meeting details (title, time, agenda)

Join meetings via the public meeting join page

Generate meeting agendas with AI assistance

Download meeting details as calendar files (ICS format)

Who this applies to
All authenticated users can view meetings for their projects. Creating and managing meetings requires a maintainer, board-member, or executive-director persona. The public meeting join page (/meetings/:id) is accessible without authentication.

Navigation
Go to app.lfx.dev and select Meetings from the left navigation sidebar. The meetings dashboard (route: /meetings) shows upcoming and recent meetings for your active project context. Tabs: Upcoming, Past, Pending RSVP. Empty state: "No upcoming meetings / Meetings from your committees and projects will appear here."

Key concepts

Meeting dashboard: The list view of all meetings for your project

Meeting join page: A public URL where attendees can join a meeting without signing in

Meeting not found page: Shown when a meeting ID does not match any known meeting

Calendar integration: Meetings can be added to your calendar via ICS file download

Public meeting access
Each meeting has a public join link at /meetings/:id. This page is accessible without authentication and shows the meeting details and join information. Attendees do not need an LFX account to view the join page.

Related sections

Committees — committees hold regular meetings

Documents — meeting agendas and notes may be stored as documents

Events — for public LFX conferences and events
meetings.tags: meetings schedule calendar join zoom virtual

meetings/faq.title: Meetings FAQ
meetings/faq.headings: Meetings FAQ Who can create meetings? Does an attendee need an LFX account to join a meeting? What is the AI agenda generation feature? Can I schedule recurring meetings? How do I add a meeting to my calendar? What happens when I open a meeting link and see "Meeting Not Found"? Can I protect a meeting with a passcode? Why can't I see a meeting I was invited to?
meetings/faq.body:
Meetings FAQ
Who can create meetings?
Users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas can create and manage meetings. Contributors can view meeting details but cannot create or edit meetings.

Does an attendee need an LFX account to join a meeting?
No. Each meeting has a public join page at /meetings/:id that is accessible without authentication. You can share this link with anyone — they do not need an LFX account to view the meeting details.

What is the AI agenda generation feature?
When creating or editing a meeting, you can use AI assistance to generate a draft agenda from a prompt. The AI service uses the LiteLLM proxy to produce agenda suggestions that you can review and edit before saving.

Can I schedule recurring meetings?
Recurring meeting options are available in the meeting creation form. Contact your project administrator for details on recurrence settings.

How do I add a meeting to my calendar?
From the meeting detail page, download the meeting as an ICS (iCalendar) file. Open the file in any calendar application — such as Google Calendar, Outlook, or Apple Calendar — to add the meeting.

What happens when I open a meeting link and see "Meeting Not Found"?
The meeting ID in the link may be incorrect, or the meeting may have been removed. Contact your meeting organizer for the correct link.

Can I protect a meeting with a passcode?
If a meeting requires a passcode, you will be prompted to enter it when joining. Contact the meeting organizer if you need the passcode.

Why can't I see a meeting I was invited to?
If you are looking for a meeting in the meetings dashboard, make sure you have the correct project context selected in the lens switcher. Meetings are scoped to a project. If you have only the join link, open it directly in your browser — no account or project context is needed.

meetings/join-meeting.title: Join a Meeting
meetings/join-meeting.headings: Join a Meeting Steps No account required Meeting not found Passcode-protected meetings Add to your calendar Related
meetings/join-meeting.body:
Join a Meeting
Any project meeting hosted in LFX Self Serve has a public join page that does not require an LFX account. This page shows the meeting details and provides the information you need to join.

Steps

Open the meeting join link shared by your meeting organizer. The link follows the format: https://app.lfx.dev/meetings/{meeting-id}.

Select the meeting to open its detail page and use the join link provided. You may need a passcode if the meeting organizer has enabled passcode protection.

No account required
The meeting join page (/meetings/:id) is publicly accessible without authentication. You do not need an LFX account to view meeting details or access the join link.

Meeting not found
If you open a meeting link and see the Meeting Not Found page, the meeting ID may be incorrect or the meeting may have been removed. Contact the meeting organizer for the correct link.

Passcode-protected meetings
If a meeting requires a passcode, you will be prompted to enter it when joining. Contact the meeting organizer if you need the passcode.

Add to your calendar
Check the meeting detail page for calendar download options.

Related

Meetings overview — an overview of the Meetings section

Schedule a Meeting — for organizers creating a new meeting

meetings/manage-meetings.title: Manage Meetings
meetings/manage-meetings.headings: Manage Meetings View meetings Edit a meeting Cancel or delete a meeting Download a meeting as a calendar file Share the meeting join link Switch project context Related
meetings/manage-meetings.body:
Manage Meetings
This article applies to users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas. Use this guide to update or cancel existing meetings.

View meetings

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Select Meetings from the left navigation sidebar.

The meetings dashboard lists upcoming and past meetings for your active project context. Tabs: Upcoming, Past, Pending RSVP. If no meetings are scheduled, the page shows "No upcoming meetings / Meetings from your committees and projects will appear here."

Select a meeting to open its detail view.

Edit a meeting
Select a meeting to open its detail view. Use the available options to edit meeting details or cancel the meeting.

Cancel or delete a meeting
Select a meeting to open its detail view. Use the available options to edit meeting details or cancel the meeting.

Download a meeting as a calendar file
From the meeting detail page, select the option to download the meeting as an ICS file. This allows you to add the meeting to any calendar application that supports the ICS format.

Share the meeting join link
Each meeting has a unique public URL at /meetings/:id. Copy this link from the meeting detail page and share it with attendees. No LFX account is required to access the join page.

Switch project context
Meetings are scoped to a project. Use the lens switcher to change your active project context. The meetings list updates automatically.

Related

Schedule a Meeting — create a new meeting

Join a Meeting — how attendees access the join page

meetings/schedule-meeting.title: Schedule a Meeting
meetings/schedule-meeting.headings: Schedule a Meeting Before you begin Steps After scheduling Add the meeting to your calendar Related
meetings/schedule-meeting.body:
Schedule a Meeting
This article applies to users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas. Use this workflow to create a new meeting for your project group.

Before you begin
Confirm you have the correct project and group selected. Meetings are associated with a specific project committee or group. The active project context in your lens switcher determines which project the meeting is linked to.

Steps

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Select My Meetings from the left navigation.

Select the option to schedule a new meeting.

Fill in the meeting details, including title, date, time, and any recurrence or conferencing settings.

Save the meeting.

After scheduling
The meeting appears on the Upcoming tab of My Meetings. Invited participants will see it in their meetings list.

Add the meeting to your calendar
After saving, download the meeting as an ICS file to add it to your calendar application. The ICS file includes the meeting title, time, and join link.

Related

Manage Meetings — edit or cancel existing meetings

Join a Meeting — how attendees join from the public link

profile.title: Profile
profile.headings: Profile What you can do Who this applies to Navigation Related sections
profile.body:
Profile
The Profile section is where you manage your personal account information on LFX Self Serve. Your profile controls how you appear to other community members and how you receive communications from the Linux Foundation.

What you can do

View your profile overview

Edit your name, photo, and personal details

Manage your email addresses

Change your password

View and update your project affiliations

Access developer settings

Who this applies to
All authenticated users have a profile. Every user can view and edit their own profile.

Navigation
Go to app.lfx.dev and select Profile from the left navigation sidebar, or navigate directly to /profile. The profile section uses a tabbed layout with the following tabs:

Tab
Route
Description

Overview
/profile
Summary of your profile and activity

Edit Profile
/profile/manage
Edit your name, photo, and personal details

Affiliations
/profile/affiliations
View and manage your project affiliations

Developer
/profile/developer
Developer-specific settings and API access

Email
/profile/email
Manage your email addresses

Password
/profile/password
Change your account password

Related sections

Settings — application-level preferences

Badges — view your earned credentials

Trainings — view your enrolled training programs
profile.tags: profile account email password affiliations developer

profile/edit-profile.title: Edit Your Profile
profile/edit-profile.headings: Edit Your Profile Profile tabs Edit your personal details Manage affiliations Manage email addresses Change your password Developer settings (API token) Related
profile/edit-profile.body:
Edit Your Profile
Use the Profile section to keep your account information current. Your profile is visible to other community members and is used in communications from the Linux Foundation.

Profile tabs
The Profile page (/profile) has three tabs:

Work history & Affiliations — your work history and project affiliations

Identities — connected accounts used to identify you and attribute contributions

Individual Enrollment — enroll in the Linux Foundation Individual Supporter plan

Edit your personal details

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Select Profile from the left navigation sidebar.

Update your fields:
First name and last name

Profile photo — upload a new image

Save your changes.

Manage affiliations

Go to Profile (/profile) and select the Work history & Affiliations tab.

View your current project affiliations.

Manage email addresses
Email address management is in Settings (/settings), not on the Profile page.

Go to Settings (/settings) > Email Settings > Add New Email Address.

A 6-digit verification code will be sent to the new address.

Enter the code to confirm the new address.

Change your password
Password management is in Settings (/settings), not on the Profile page.

Go to Settings (/settings) > Password > Change Password.

Fill in Current Password, New Password, and Confirm New Password.

Select Change Password to apply.

Developer settings (API token)
Developer settings are in Settings (/settings), not on the Profile page.

Go to Settings (/settings) > Developer Settings.

Your Personal Access Token is listed here.

Use the Show button to reveal it and the Copy button to copy it.

Never share this token publicly.

Related

Profile overview — an overview of the Profile section

Settings — email, password, and API token management

profile/faq.title: Profile FAQ
profile/faq.headings: Profile FAQ How do I change my email address? How do I change my password? How do I update my profile photo? What are affiliations? Where are developer settings (API token)? My name is displaying incorrectly. How do I fix it? Who do I contact if I cannot access my account?
profile/faq.body:
Profile FAQ
How do I change my email address?
Email addresses are managed in Settings (/settings), not on the Profile page. Go to Settings > Email Settings > Add New Email Address. A 6-digit verification code will be sent to the new address to confirm it.

How do I change my password?
Go to Settings (/settings) > Password > Change Password. Fill in Current Password, New Password, and Confirm New Password, then select Change Password.

How do I update my profile photo?
To update your profile photo, look for the photo area on your Profile page and select it to upload a new image.

What are affiliations?
Affiliations are the Linux Foundation projects you are associated with. They appear on your profile and may affect your persona and the lenses available to you. Go to Profile and select the Work history & Affiliations tab to view and manage them.

Where are developer settings (API token)?
Developer settings (API token) are in Settings (/settings) > Developer Settings. Use the Show button to reveal your Personal Access Token and the Copy button to copy it.

My name is displaying incorrectly. How do I fix it?
Go to Profile (/profile) and update your first and last name fields and save your changes.

Who do I contact if I cannot access my account?
Contact LFX support through the Help Center. If you cannot sign in at all, use the login page's help or forgotten password link.

settings.title: Account Settings
settings.headings: Account Settings What you can do Who this applies to Navigation Related sections
settings.body:
Account Settings
The Settings section lets you manage your account preferences within LFX Self Serve. Settings are separate from your profile information and cover email addresses, password and two-factor authentication, and developer API token settings.

What you can do

Add and verify email addresses

Change your password or send a reset link

View and copy your Personal Access Token (API token)

Who this applies to
All authenticated users have access to the Settings section.

Navigation
Go to app.lfx.dev and select Settings from the left navigation sidebar. The settings dashboard (route: /settings) shows all available configuration options.

Related sections

Profile — for personal account information such as name, photo, and email

Dashboard — lens and persona controls are in the top navigation, not Settings

settings/faq.title: Settings FAQ
settings/faq.headings: Settings FAQ What is the difference between Settings and Profile? Does LFX Self Serve have notification preferences? Are my settings shared across devices? Who do I contact if a setting isn't working?
settings/faq.body:
Settings FAQ
What is the difference between Settings and Profile?
Settings (/settings) manages your account security and access credentials — email addresses, password, two-factor authentication, and your developer API token. Profile (/profile) manages your personal information — such as your name, work history, affiliations, and connected identities. Both are accessible from the left navigation.

Does LFX Self Serve have notification preferences?
LFX Self Serve does not currently include a notification preferences section in Settings. Email and password settings are managed at /settings.

Are my settings shared across devices?
Account settings (email, password, API token) are tied to your LFX account and apply across all devices.

Who do I contact if a setting isn't working?
Contact LFX support through the Help Center. Describe the setting you changed and the behavior you expected.

settings/manage-settings.title: Manage Settings
settings/manage-settings.headings: Manage Settings Access your settings Available settings Save changes Related
settings/manage-settings.body:
Manage Settings
Use the Settings section to configure your application preferences in LFX Self Serve.

Access your settings

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Select Settings from the left navigation sidebar.

The settings dashboard (route: /settings) displays your current configuration options.

Available settings
The Settings page (/settings) has three sections on one page:

Email Settings — add a new email address using the Add New Email Address button, then confirm with the Send Code button. A 6-digit verification code is sent to the new address.

Password — change your password using the Current Password, New Password, and Confirm New Password fields, then select Change Password. Use Send Reset Link if you have forgotten your current password. An Account Recovery section is also available.

Developer Settings — view your Personal Access Token. Use the Show button to reveal it and the Copy button to copy it to your clipboard.

Save changes
Settings are NOT auto-saved. Each section has its own action button: Add New Email Address / Send Code for email, Change Password for password, and Show / Copy for the API token. Two-factor authentication settings display the message "Two-factor authentication settings are currently unavailable" with a Set up 2FA button.

Related

Settings overview — an overview of the Settings section

Profile — personal account information is managed separately in Profile

surveys.title: Surveys
surveys.headings: Surveys What you can do Navigation Key concepts Related sections
surveys.body:
Surveys
Creating and managing surveys requires a maintainer, board-member, or executive-director persona. Contributors can respond to surveys they receive but cannot create them.

The Surveys section lets you create surveys for your project community, collect responses, and analyze feedback including Net Promoter Score (NPS) data. Surveys are a tool for gathering structured input from contributors, maintainers, and stakeholders.

What you can do

Create new surveys for your project

Edit existing surveys

Collect and review responses

View NPS analytics for your project

Share surveys with respondents via a survey link

Navigation
Go to app.lfx.dev and select Surveys from the left navigation sidebar. The surveys dashboard (route: /surveys) shows all surveys for your active project context. Use the tabs to filter: All, Open, Closed.

Key concepts

Net Promoter Score (NPS): A metric measuring how likely respondents are to recommend your project on a scale of 0–10

Survey: A set of questions sent to a defined audience for structured feedback collection

Related sections

Votes — for formal decision-making polls rather than feedback surveys

Committees — committee members are a common survey audience

surveys/create-survey.title: Create a Survey
surveys/create-survey.headings: Create a Survey Before you begin Steps After creation Permissions Related
surveys/create-survey.body:
Create a Survey
This article applies to users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas. Use this guide to create a new survey for your project community.

Before you begin
Confirm you have the correct project selected in the lens switcher. The new survey will be associated with the currently active project context.

Steps

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Select My Surveys from the left navigation.

Select the option to create a new survey.

Fill in the survey details, including title, description, questions, and any date or audience settings.

Save or publish the survey to make it available to respondents.

After creation
The survey appears on the My Surveys page. Respondents can access it via the survey link.

Permissions
Only users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas can create surveys. If you do not see the create option, contact your project administrator.

Related

Manage Surveys — edit and analyze existing surveys

Votes — for formal decision-making polls rather than feedback surveys

surveys/faq.title: Surveys FAQ
surveys/faq.headings: Surveys FAQ Who can create a survey? What is NPS and how does it work? How do respondents access a survey? Can I see individual survey responses? Can I edit a survey after it has been published? How do I see which surveys are still open? Why don't I see any surveys? What is the difference between Surveys and Votes?
surveys/faq.body:
Surveys FAQ
Who can create a survey?
Users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas can create surveys. Contributors can respond to surveys they receive.

What is NPS and how does it work?
Net Promoter Score (NPS) measures how likely respondents are to recommend your project on a scale of 0 to 10. Respondents scoring 9–10 are "promoters," 7–8 are "passives," and 0–6 are "detractors." The NPS is calculated as the percentage of promoters minus the percentage of detractors.

How do respondents access a survey?
Survey links are shared by the survey creator with respondents directly.

Can I see individual survey responses?
Select a survey to view its responses and results. Use the survey settings to edit or close it.

Can I edit a survey after it has been published?
Contact your project administrator to make changes to a published survey.

How do I see which surveys are still open?
Use the Open and Closed tabs on the My Surveys page to filter surveys by status.

Why don't I see any surveys?
Make sure you have the correct project selected in the lens switcher. Surveys are scoped to a project context.

What is the difference between Surveys and Votes?
Surveys collect general feedback and opinions from a broad audience. Votes are formal decision-making polls used in governance contexts, such as committee elections or policy decisions.

surveys/manage-surveys.title: Manage Surveys
surveys/manage-surveys.headings: Manage Surveys View surveys View survey responses View NPS analytics Edit a survey Close or deactivate a survey Switch project context Related
surveys/manage-surveys.body:
Manage Surveys
This article applies to users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas. Use this guide to view, edit, and analyze your project surveys.

View surveys

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Select Surveys from the left navigation sidebar.

The surveys dashboard lists all surveys for your active project context.

Select a survey to open its detail view.

View survey responses
Select a survey to open its detail view and see response data.

View NPS analytics
The Net Promoter Score (NPS) analytics show how likely respondents are to recommend your project.

Edit a survey

Open the survey you want to update.

Select the edit option (route: /surveys/manage with the existing survey loaded).

Update the survey title, questions, or settings.

Save your changes.

Close or deactivate a survey
Select a survey to view its responses and results. Use the survey settings to edit or close it.

Switch project context
Surveys are scoped to a project. Use the lens switcher to change your active project context. The surveys list updates automatically.

Related

Create a Survey — steps to create a new survey

Surveys overview — an overview of the Surveys section

trainings.title: Training & Certifications
trainings.headings: Training & Certifications What you can do Who this applies to Navigation Relationship to badges Related sections
trainings.body:
Training & Certifications
The Training & Certifications section shows your Linux Foundation training enrollments and certifications. Training programs are offered through the Linux Foundation's learning platform and help you develop skills recognized by the open source community.

What you can do

View your enrolled training courses

Check your enrollment and completion status

Access your earned certifications and rewards

Training enrollment is handled on the Linux Foundation training platform. LFX Self Serve displays your enrolled courses and certifications in read-only view. Use the LF Training Portal link on the Useful links tab to access your courses.

Who this applies to
All authenticated users can view their training enrollments.

Navigation
Go to app.lfx.dev and select Training & Certifications from the left navigation sidebar (route: /me/training). The page shows tabs for Certifications, Enrolled Trainings, Rewards, and Useful links.

Relationship to badges
Completing certain training programs qualifies you for LFX badges. After you complete a qualifying course, the badge appears automatically in your Badges section.

Related sections

Badges — credentials earned from completed training programs

Transactions — training purchases appear in your transaction history

trainings/faq.title: Trainings FAQ
trainings/faq.headings: Trainings FAQ How do I enroll in a training course? Why don't I see a training I enrolled in? How do I see my training completion certificates? Do training enrollments expire? Can I earn a badge by completing a training course? Who do I contact if my training status is incorrect? Where can I find a list of available training courses?
trainings/faq.body:
Trainings FAQ
How do I enroll in a training course?
Enrollment is handled on the Linux Foundation training platform (training.linuxfoundation.org). LFX Self Serve shows your enrolled courses in read-only view. Go to the Useful links tab on the Training & Certifications page and select LF Training Portal to access the training catalog.

Why don't I see a training I enrolled in?
There may be a delay of up to 24 hours between enrolling on the training platform and the enrollment appearing in LFX Self Serve. If a course is still missing after 24 hours, contact LFX support.

How do I see my training completion certificates?
Certificates earned through Linux Foundation training appear on the Certifications tab in LFX Self Serve. You can also access them directly from the LF Training Portal.

Do training enrollments expire?
Training enrollment expiry, if applicable, is shown in the LF Training Portal. Check your course details there for any time limits.

Can I earn a badge by completing a training course?
Yes. Completing certain Linux Foundation training programs qualifies you for LFX badges. The badge appears automatically in your Badges section after you complete a qualifying course.

Who do I contact if my training status is incorrect?
Contact LFX support through the Help Center. Include the course name and your expected completion status.

Where can I find a list of available training courses?
Visit the Linux Foundation training catalog at https://training.linuxfoundation.org. You can also use the LF Training Portal link on the Useful links tab of the Training & Certifications page.

trainings/manage-trainings.title: Manage Trainings
trainings/manage-trainings.headings: Manage Trainings View your training enrollments What the trainings page shows Resume or enroll in a training Empty state Related
trainings/manage-trainings.body:
Manage Trainings
Use the Trainings section to view your Linux Foundation training enrollments and track your progress.

View your training enrollments

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Select Training & Certifications from the left navigation sidebar (route: /me/training).

Your enrolled training courses appear under the Enrolled Trainings tab.

Select a training to view its details.

What the trainings page shows
Stats displayed at the top: Certificates Earned, Enrolled Trainings, Completed Trainings.

Tabs:

Certifications — certifications you have earned

Enrolled Trainings — courses you are currently enrolled in

Rewards — rewards associated with your learning activity

Useful links — links to the LF Training Portal, LF Education & Certification, and Certification Verification Tool

Resume or enroll in a training
Enrollment in new training courses is handled on the Linux Foundation training platform, not within LFX Self Serve. To resume a course or enroll in a new one, go to the Useful links tab and select LF Training Portal to open https://training.linuxfoundation.org.

Empty state
If you have no training enrollments, the page shows a message indicating no trainings are available. To enroll in courses, visit https://training.linuxfoundation.org.

Related

Badges — training completions can earn you LFX badges

Transactions — training purchases appear in your billing history

transactions.title: My Transactions
transactions.headings: My Transactions What you can do Who this applies to Navigation Related sections
transactions.body:
My Transactions
The My Transactions section shows your Linux Foundation purchase history. Recent purchases may take up to 48 hours to appear. This includes payments for training courses, certifications, event registrations, and other LFX purchases.

What you can do

View your complete purchase history

See transaction details including amount, date, and item purchased

Filter by transaction type using tabs: All Transactions, Event Tickets, Training, Certifications, Bundles, Subscriptions, Individual Support

Access receipt or invoice options from the transaction detail view

Who this applies to
All authenticated users can view their own transactions. Each user sees only their own billing history.

Navigation
Go to app.lfx.dev and select My Transactions from the left navigation sidebar (route: /me/transactions). The transactions page lists all your purchases associated with your LFX account.

Related sections

Trainings — training purchases appear as transactions

Profile — your account information associated with billing

transactions/faq.title: Transactions FAQ
transactions/faq.headings: Transactions FAQ What purchases appear in my transaction history? Can I download a receipt or invoice? Why is a purchase missing from my transaction history? Can I request a refund through LFX Self Serve? Who can see my transactions? I was charged incorrectly. Who do I contact?
transactions/faq.body:
Transactions FAQ
What purchases appear in my transaction history?
Your transaction history includes all purchases made through the LFX platform under your account, such as training course enrollments, certification exams, and event registrations.

Can I download a receipt or invoice?
Receipt availability varies by transaction type. Select a transaction to see available options.

Why is a purchase missing from my transaction history?
Recent purchases may take up to 48 hours to appear in LFX Self Serve. If a purchase is still missing after 48 hours, contact LFX support with your order confirmation.

Can I request a refund through LFX Self Serve?
To request a refund, contact LFX support with your order confirmation.

Who can see my transactions?
You can only see your own transactions. Other users and project administrators cannot access your billing history.

I was charged incorrectly. Who do I contact?
Contact LFX support through the Help Center. Include the transaction date, amount, and item name so the support team can investigate.

transactions/view-transactions.title: View Transactions
transactions/view-transactions.headings: View Transactions Steps What a transaction record shows Filter transactions Download receipts or invoices Empty state Related
transactions/view-transactions.body:
View Transactions
Use the Transactions section to review your purchase and billing history on the LFX platform.

Steps

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Select My Transactions from the left navigation sidebar (route: /me/transactions).

Your transaction history appears in the main content area.

Select a transaction to view its details.

What a transaction record shows

Item name and description (for example, a training course or event registration)

Transaction date

Amount charged

Select a transaction to view its details. Receipt or invoice download options, if available, appear in the transaction detail view.

Filter transactions
Use the tabs at the top of the page to filter by transaction type: All Transactions, Event Tickets, Training, Certifications, Bundles, Subscriptions, Individual Support.

Download receipts or invoices
Select a transaction to view its details. Receipt or invoice download options, if available, appear in the transaction detail view.

Empty state
If you have no transactions, the page displays a message indicating no purchase history is available. Transactions are recorded only when you make a purchase through the LFX platform.

Related

Transactions overview — an overview of the Transactions section

Trainings — training purchases are listed as transactions

votes.title: Votes
votes.headings: Votes What you can do Navigation Key concepts Project context Related sections
votes.body:
Votes
All authenticated users can view polls and cast votes on polls they are eligible for. Creating and managing polls requires a maintainer, board-member, or executive-director persona.

The Votes section lets you create and manage polls for formal project governance decisions. Votes are used for committee elections, policy approvals, and other decisions that require a recorded community response.

What you can do

View all polls for your project or foundation

Create new polls with custom options and voting rules

Cast your vote on open polls

View real-time and final vote results

Manage poll settings and close polls

Navigation
Go to app.lfx.dev and select Votes from the left navigation sidebar. The votes dashboard (route: /votes) lists all polls for your active project context. Use the tabs to filter: All, Active, Draft, Ended.

Key concepts

Poll: A formal vote with defined options and an eligible voter set

Voter eligibility: Voter eligibility is configured by the poll creator.

Vote result: The tally of responses after the poll closes

Project context
Votes are scoped to a specific project or foundation. Use the lens switcher to change your active context and see votes for a different project.

Related sections

Committees — committee members are often the eligible voter set for polls

Surveys — for collecting general feedback rather than formal decisions

votes/create-vote.title: Create a Vote
votes/create-vote.headings: Create a Vote Before you begin Steps After creation Permissions Related
votes/create-vote.body:
Create a Vote
This article applies to users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas. Use this guide to create a new poll for your project's governance decisions.

Before you begin
Confirm you have the correct project selected in the lens switcher. The new poll will be associated with the currently active project context.

Steps

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Select My Votes from the left navigation.

Select the option to create a new poll.

Fill in the poll details, including title, description, voting options, end date, and voter eligibility settings.

Save or publish the poll.

After creation
The poll appears in the My Votes dashboard with an Active status. Eligible voters can cast their votes until the poll closes.

Permissions
Only users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas can create polls. Contributors can view and participate in polls they are eligible for but cannot create new ones.

Related

Manage Votes — edit, close, and view results for polls

Committees — committee members are often the voter set for governance polls

votes/faq.title: Votes FAQ
votes/faq.headings: Votes FAQ Who can create a poll? Who can vote in a poll? Can I change my vote after submitting? Are votes anonymous? How do I see the poll results? What is the difference between Votes and Surveys? Can a poll be extended after it closes? Why can't I see a poll I should be able to vote in? Who do I contact if there is an error with a vote result?
votes/faq.body:
Votes FAQ
Who can create a poll?
Users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas can create polls. Contributors can participate in polls they are eligible to vote in but cannot create new ones.

Who can vote in a poll?
Voter eligibility and anonymity settings are configured when the poll is created. Contact your project administrator for details on your specific poll setup.

Can I change my vote after submitting?
Vote change availability depends on the poll configuration. Check the poll detail view for options after submitting.

Are votes anonymous?
Voter eligibility and anonymity settings are configured when the poll is created. Contact your project administrator for details on your specific poll setup.

How do I see the poll results?
Vote results are visible within the poll detail view. The display format depends on the poll configuration.

What is the difference between Votes and Surveys?
Votes are formal governance polls with defined voter eligibility, specific options, and recorded decisions. Surveys collect general feedback and opinions from a broader audience. Use Votes for committee decisions and elections; use Surveys for community feedback.

Can a poll be extended after it closes?
Contact your project administrator if you need to reopen or extend a closed poll.

Why can't I see a poll I should be able to vote in?
Make sure you have the correct project selected in the lens switcher. Polls are scoped to a project or foundation context. If you believe you should be eligible to vote in a specific poll, contact your project administrator.

Who do I contact if there is an error with a vote result?
Contact LFX support through the Help Center. Include the poll name, the project, and a description of the discrepancy.

votes/manage-votes.title: Manage Votes
votes/manage-votes.headings: Manage Votes View polls Cast your vote View results Edit a poll Close a poll Switch project context Related
votes/manage-votes.body:
Manage Votes
This article applies to users with maintainer, board-member, or executive-director personas. Use this guide to view, update, and manage polls in your project.

View polls

Sign in to app.lfx.dev.

Select Votes from the left navigation sidebar.

The votes dashboard lists all polls for your active project context.

Select a poll to open its detail view.

Cast your vote

Open an open poll from the votes dashboard.

Select your preferred option or options from the poll.

Submit your vote.

View results
Select a poll to view its current results and status.

Edit a poll

Open the poll you want to update.

Select the edit option on the poll detail page.

Update the poll details and save your changes.

Close a poll
Use the poll settings to edit details or close the poll before its end date.

Switch project context
Polls are scoped to a project or foundation. Use the lens switcher to change your active context.
